areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: The All-CNN-C model is implemented exactly as in Table 2 (Section 3.1): every max-pooling layer of base model C is replaced by a 3x3 convolu
leaves.why: code/_best_attempt/models.py build_all_cnn (letter='C', variant='allcnn') replaces each max-pool with a 3×3 stride-2 conv whose out-channels equal in-channels (96→96, 192→192), uses only conv+ReLU layers, and AllCNNModel.forward applies the conv(192,10,1×1) head followed by spati

leaves.label: The three base models A, B, and C from Table 1 (Section 3.1) are implemented: model B as the Network-in-Network variant with one 1-by-1 conv
leaves.why: models.py implements letter A (single 5×5 conv per block), letter B (5×5 conv followed by a 1×1 NiN conv per block), and letter C (two 3×3 convs per block replacing the 5×5), all sharing the conv(192,3×3)+conv(192,1×1)+conv(num_classes,1×1)+GAP head, matching Table 1; completed b

leaves.label: The Strided-CNN-C variant (Table 2, Section 3.1) is implemented by removing max-pooling and increasing the stride of the convolution layer p
leaves.why: models.py letter='C', variant='strided' removes the MaxPool and sets stride=2 on the conv immediately preceding it (second 3×3 conv per block), per Table 2; measured run a_strided/c_strided cells confirm the variant trained (metrics.json a_strided test_error_pct=15.08).

leaves.label: The ConvPool-CNN-C variant (Table 2, Section 3.1) is implemented by placing a dense convolution layer, with the same kernel size as the resp
leaves.why: models.py variant='convpool' inserts an extra dense conv (kernel 3, same size as the 3×3 pooling kernel from _maxpool()) before each retained MaxPool(3,stride=2), exactly the Table 2 ConvPool control; outputs/.../a_convpool_cifar10_noaug and b_convpool cells exist.

leaves.label: Guided backpropagation is implemented as defined in Section 4 / Figure 1: when backpropagating through a ReLU, values are masked to zero whe
leaves.why: guided_backprop.py GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook computes grad * (input>0).float() * (grad>0).float() — the combined standard-backprop + deconvnet mask of Section 4 — by replacing every nn.ReLU, with no pooling-switch mechanism anywhere in the implementation.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 (Krizhevsky & Hinton, 2009) images are whitened and contrast normalized following Goodfellow et al. (2013), as stated
leaves.why: data.py implements per-sample GCN (_gcn, scale=55) followed by ZCA whitening (compute_zca_matrix, epsilon=0.1, fit on training data) applied to both CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 via load_cifar(use_zca=True), explicitly following Goodfellow et al. (2013); the c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell

leaves.label: The data-augmentation runs use exactly the augmentations of Section 3.2: horizontally flipped copies of all images plus random translations
leaves.why: data.py _augment implements horizontal flip plus random translation of at most ±5 pixels (pad=5 then crop) and nothing else, matching Section 3.2 except for the online-vs-offline flip detail.

leaves.label: The CIFAR-100 experiment reuses the identical All-CNN-C model and identical data pipeline and hyperparameters (learning rate and its schedul
leaves.why: The same models.py builder (num_classes=100) and the same data.py GCN+ZCA pipeline serve CIFAR-100, and a completed c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell exists (metrics.json, model.pt, training_curves.json), but identical-hyperparameter reuse is not directly verifiable.

leaves.label: Training logs/configs show all CIFAR networks trained with stochastic gradient descent with fixed momentum 0.9 for a total of 350 epochs, wi
leaves.why: metrics.json shows epochs_total=350/epochs_run=350 for a_base and a_strided, and the measured train_loss_history exhibits the characteristic step drops at exactly epochs ~200 (0.674→0.558) and ~250 (0.520→0.498) consistent with the ×0.1 schedule S=[200,250,300].

leaves.label: Run artifacts show the learning rate γ was searched per model over exactly the fixed set {0.25, 0.1, 0.05, 0.01}, with results reported for
leaves.why: metrics.json records lr=0.05 and best_lr=0.05 (a value within the paper's set) for a_base/a_strided, indicating an LR-selection mechanism, but the searched set and per-γ results are not evidenced.

leaves.label: Configs/code apply dropout with probability 20% on the input image and 50% after each pooling layer (or after the layer replacing pooling),
leaves.why: models.py build_all_cnn applies nn.Dropout(0.2) on the input and nn.Dropout(0.5) after each pooling layer or its replacement in every variant, matching Section 3.2; the weight-decay setting is not visible.

leaves.label: The ImageNet run trains the upscaled All-CNN-B with 12 convolutional layers (conv1–conv12, Table 6 in the Appendix) for 450,000 iterations w
leaves.why: models.py _ImageNetAllCNNB implements the 12-conv Table 6 architecture, but the required training run and its hyperparameters are entirely unexecuted.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 results are reported as test error (%) on the standard held-out test sets, separately for the no-augmentation and aug
leaves.why: metrics.json reports test_error_pct on the held-out test set (e.g. model_a_cifar10_test_error=15.61, a_strided=15.08) keyed per model under per_dataset['CIFAR-10'], with separate *_noaug and *_aug cells (including c_allcnn_cifar10_aug and c_allcnn_cifar100_aug) mirroring the Tabl

leaves.label: ILSVRC-2012 performance is measured as Top-1 validation error evaluated only on the center 224x224 patch of each validation image, matching
leaves.why: No measured ImageNet Top-1 validation error or center-crop evaluation code exists anywhere in the evidence.

leaves.label: Class probabilities at evaluation are produced by the paper's fully convolutional inference path (Section 2): the topmost 1-by-1 convolution
leaves.why: models.py AllCNNModel.forward runs the fully convolutional body ending in a 1×1 conv producing num_classes outputs per spatial position, then averages over all positions (x.mean(dim=[2,3])) before the softmax/argmax — no fully connected layer exists in any model, and the measured

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: Reproduced base model A achieves approximately 12.5% test error on CIFAR-10 without data augmentation, matching the result reported in Secti
leaves.why: metrics.json records a_base cifar10_noaug test_error_pct=15.61 (350/350 epochs, lr=0.05), a trained but substantially worse result than the required ~12.5% ±1%, so the specific magnitude claim of this leaf is not met.

leaves.label: The reproduced metrics reproduce the qualitative ordering of Table 3 (Section 3.2): Strided-CNN-C performs worse than base model C, while Al
leaves.why: The provided metrics.json contains no measured results for Model C base / ConvPool-CNN-C / All-CNN-C on CIFAR-10 no-aug (their output dirs hold only metrics.json.tmp), so the claimed ordering is unsupported by any measured metric.

leaves.label: Reproduced All-CNN-C test error on CIFAR-10 (with and/or without augmentation) is at or below the corresponding base-model-C error, reproduc
leaves.why: There is no measured All-CNN-C vs base-Model-C pair anywhere in metrics.json or the completed cell outputs, so the paper's central claim cannot be confirmed from measured evidence.

leaves.label: The reproduced ImageNet All-CNN-B achieves a Top-1 validation error close to 41.2% on the ILSVRC-2012 center 224x224 evaluation, with a mode
leaves.why: models.py contains an ImageNet All-CNN-B architecture stub but the docstring states it was not run and metrics.json carries no ImageNet metric, so this result-match leaf has zero measured support.

leaves.label: The repository contains runnable source code and configs for every evaluated variant — base models A/B/C, Strided-CNN-A/B/C, ConvPool-CNN-A/
leaves.why: models.py:build_all_cnn implements all 12 variants (A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn) with nn.Dropout(0.2) input and nn.Dropout(0.5) per block, output cells exist for all 12 in outputs/, and the a_base train_loss_history in metrics.json shows step drops at epochs ~200 and ~25

leaves.label: Per-run training logs and a machine-readable metrics file (e.g. metrics.json) record the final test error for each model variant on CIFAR-10
leaves.why: Completed cells (e.g. a_base, a_strided, b_base, c_allcnn_cifar100_aug) each have per-cell .log, metrics.json, config_used.json and training_curves.json with lr/best_lr fields (metrics.json: a_base lr=0.05, test_error_pct=15.61), satisfying traceability, but coverage of 'each mod

leaves.label: Saved visualization artifacts exist for the guided-backpropagation method of Section 4: reconstructed feature visualizations for lower layer
leaves.why: code/guided_backprop.py genuinely implements the Section-4 guided-backprop rule (GuidedBackpropReLU masking grad where input<0 or grad<0) and a run_guided_backprop saver, but no produced visualization artifact is visible in outputs/ and the layer-wise Figure 2/3 protocol is not i

leaf_scores.justification: metrics.json records a_base cifar10_noaug test_error_pct=15.61 (350/350 epochs, lr=0.05), a trained but substantially worse result than the required ~12.5% ±1%, so the specific magnitude claim of this leaf is not met.

The provided metrics.json contains no measured results for Model C base / ConvPool-CNN-C / All-CNN-C on CIFAR-10 no-aug (their output dirs hold only metrics.json.tmp), so the claimed ordering is unsupported by any measured metric.
There is no measured All-CNN-C vs base-Model-C pair anywhere in metrics.json or the completed cell outputs, so the paper's central claim cannot be confirmed from measured evidence.
models.py contains an ImageNet All-CNN-B architecture stub but the docstring states it was not run and metrics.json carries no ImageNet metric, so this result-match leaf has zero measured support.

models.py:build_all_cnn implements all 12 variants (A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn) with nn.Dropout(0.2) input and nn.Dropout(0.5) per block, output cells exist for all 12 in outputs/, and the a_base train_loss_history in metrics.json shows step drops at epochs ~200 and ~250 (0.674→0.558, 0.520→0.498) consistent with the [200,250,300] schedule over epochs_total=350 — only momentum/weight-decay visibility in configs is unconfirmed.

Completed cells (e.g. a_base, a_strided, b_base, c_allcnn_cifar100_aug) each have per-cell .log, metrics.json, config_used.json and training_curves.json with lr/best_lr fields (metrics.json: a_base lr=0.05, test_error_pct=15.61), satisfying traceability, but coverage of 'each model variant' fails because ~5 CIFAR-10 cells — including base Model C and All-CNN-C no-aug — never produced final metrics.

code/guided_backprop.py genuinely implements the Section-4 guided-backprop rule (GuidedBackpropReLU masking grad where input<0 or grad<0) and a run_guided_backprop saver, but no produced visualization artifact is visible in outputs/ and the layer-wise Figure 2/3 protocol is not implemented.

code/_best_attempt/models.py build_all_cnn (letter='C', variant='allcnn') replaces each max-pool with a 3×3 stride-2 conv whose out-channels equal in-channels (96→96, 192→192), uses only conv+ReLU layers, and AllCNNModel.forward applies the conv(192,10,1×1) head followed by spatial mean (GAP) over all positions with softmax at the loss.

models.py implements letter A (single 5×5 conv per block), letter B (5×5 conv followed by a 1×1 NiN conv per block), and letter C (two 3×3 convs per block replacing the 5×5), all sharing the conv(192,3×3)+conv(192,1×1)+conv(num_classes,1×1)+GAP head, matching Table 1; completed b_base/c-variant cells exist under outputs/.

models.py letter='C', variant='strided' removes the MaxPool and sets stride=2 on the conv immediately preceding it (second 3×3 conv per block), per Table 2; measured run a_strided/c_strided cells confirm the variant trained (metrics.json a_strided test_error_pct=15.08).

models.py variant='convpool' inserts an extra dense conv (kernel 3, same size as the 3×3 pooling kernel from _maxpool()) before each retained MaxPool(3,stride=2), exactly the Table 2 ConvPool control; outputs/.../a_convpool_cifar10_noaug and b_convpool cells exist.

guided_backprop.py GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook computes grad * (input>0).float() * (grad>0).float() — the combined standard-backprop + deconvnet mask of Section 4 — by replacing every nn.ReLU, with no pooling-switch mechanism anywhere in the implementation.

data.py implements per-sample GCN (_gcn, scale=55) followed by ZCA whitening (compute_zca_matrix, epsilon=0.1, fit on training data) applied to both CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 via load_cifar(use_zca=True), explicitly following Goodfellow et al. (2013); the c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell ran through this pipeline.
data.py _augment implements horizontal flip plus random translation of at most ±5 pixels (pad=5 then crop) and nothing else, matching Section 3.2 except for the online-vs-offline flip detail.

The same models.py builder (num_classes=100) and the same data.py GCN+ZCA pipeline serve CIFAR-100, and a completed c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell exists (metrics.json, model.pt, training_curves.json), but identical-hyperparameter reuse is not directly verifiable.

metrics.json shows epochs_total=350/epochs_run=350 for a_base and a_strided, and the measured train_loss_history exhibits the characteristic step drops at exactly epochs ~200 (0.674→0.558) and ~250 (0.520→0.498) consistent with the ×0.1 schedule S=[200,250,300].
metrics.json records lr=0.05 and best_lr=0.05 (a value within the paper's set) for a_base/a_strided, indicating an LR-selection mechanism, but the searched set and per-γ results are not evidenced.

models.py build_all_cnn applies nn.Dropout(0.2) on the input and nn.Dropout(0.5) after each pooling layer or its replacement in every variant, matching Section 3.2; the weight-decay setting is not visible.
models.py _ImageNetAllCNNB implements the 12-conv Table 6 architecture, but the required training run and its hyperparameters are entirely unexecuted.

metrics.json reports test_error_pct on the held-out test set (e.g. model_a_cifar10_test_error=15.61, a_strided=15.08) keyed per model under per_dataset['CIFAR-10'], with separate *_noaug and *_aug cells (including c_allcnn_cifar10_aug and c_allcnn_cifar100_aug) mirroring the Tables 3/4 format.
No measured ImageNet Top-1 validation error or center-crop evaluation code exists anywhere in the evidence.

models.py AllCNNModel.forward runs the fully convolutional body ending in a 1×1 conv producing num_classes outputs per spatial position, then averages over all positions (x.mean(dim=[2,3])) before the softmax/argmax — no fully connected layer exists in any model, and the measured test_accuracy values in metrics.json were produced through this path.

weak_leaves.justification: The provided metrics.json contains no measured results for Model C base / ConvPool-CNN-C / All-CNN-C on CIFAR-10 no-aug (their output dirs hold only metrics.json.tmp), so the claimed ordering is unsupported by any measured metric.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: There is no measured All-CNN-C vs base-Model-C pair anywhere in metrics.json or the completed cell outputs, so the paper's central claim cannot be confirmed from measured evidence.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: models.py contains an ImageNet All-CNN-B architecture stub but the docstring states it was not run and metrics.json carries no ImageNet metric, so this result-match leaf has zero measured support.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: No measured ImageNet Top-1 validation error or center-crop evaluation code exists anywhere in the evidence.
models.py _ImageNetAllCNNB implements the 12-conv Table 6 architecture, but the required training run and its hyperparameters are entirely unexecuted.

metrics.json records a_base cifar10_noaug test_error_pct=15.61 (350/350 epochs, lr=0.05), a trained but substantially worse result than the required ~12.5% ±1%, so the specific magnitude claim of this leaf is not met.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: Completed cells (e.g. a_base, a_strided, b_base, c_allcnn_cifar100_aug) each have per-cell .log, metrics.json, config_used.json and training_curves.json with lr/best_lr fields (metrics.json: a_base lr=0.05, test_error_pct=15.61), satisfying traceability, but coverage of 'each model variant' fails because ~5 CIFAR-10 cells — including base Model C and All-CNN-C no-aug — never produced final metrics.

code/guided_backprop.py genuinely implements the Section-4 guided-backprop rule (GuidedBackpropReLU masking grad where input<0 or grad<0) and a run_guided_backprop saver, but no produced visualization artifact is visible in outputs/ and the layer-wise Figure 2/3 protocol is not implemented.
